quote:
The river that seeks to calm the storm will itself be carried farther by the current it hopes to master.  

A child who learns the drumbeat of war is too often forgotten when the ancient rhythm resurfaces.  

In the garden of a nation, the fences we build decide whose roots may sprout.  

When silence is taken hostage, the world makes noise in its own confusion.  

Even the gentlest matriarch between mountains and coast cannot shield the sudden thud of explosives.  

Counting souls in a nation is like counting stars: one asks, “Which one is yours?”  

The ash of a fallen ceremony reminds us that the faintest flame can still scorch the bone of tradition.
quote_preview: The river that seeks to calm the storm will itself be carried farther by the current it hopes to master.
